talk_title: Dual-compartment targeting GPNMB CAR T cells for glioblastoma

speaker.name: Seela Singh
speaker.affiliation: Ontario Institute for Cancer Research, Toronto, Canada

models.name: BT972-derived GSC PDX
models.genotype_or_key_features: GPNMB+ glioma stem cells, murine macrophage-deficient NOD scid mice

MOA_summary: GPNMB is co‑expressed on glioma stem cells and immunosuppressive tumor‑associated macrophages; a GPNMB‑directed CAR T cell simultaneously eliminates both compartments, leading to complete tumor clearance in orthotopic GBM models and durable survival without skin toxicity.
key_findings: GPNMB is up‑regulated in GBM and enriched on both glioma stem cells and M2‑like macrophages.
CRISPR knockout of GPNMB reduces proliferation of patient‑derived GSCs and shifts tumor cell states away from mesenchymal programs.
Intracranial delivery of 1 × 10⁶ GPNMB CAR T cells eradicated orthotopic GBM PDX tumors and cleared GPNMB‑positive macrophages.
In syngeneic GL261 mice, GPNMB CAR T induced complete remission without observable skin toxicity.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Antigen escape observed in a minority of mice with tumor recurrence.
CAR T efficacy reduced in models with low GPNMB expression on macrophages.
open_questions: Can combining GPNMB CAR T with checkpoint blockade prevent antigen escape?
What is the optimal dosing schedule to maintain efficacy while minimizing neurotoxicity?

talk_title: Macrophage‑targeted conjugates (MAC‑TACs) for pan‑solid tumor therapy
MOA_summary: A small‑molecule‑antibody conjugate binds a TAM‑restricted surface receptor, is internalized, and releases a cleavable MMAE payload within the tumor microenvironment; the non‑dividing TAMs act as drug depots, delivering cytotoxic payload to neighboring cancer cells while sparing normal tissues.
key_findings: RS5 (MMAE payload) achieved complete tumor regression in multiple subcutaneous solid‑tumor models, including doxorubicin‑resistant sarcoma PDX.
In intracranial glioblastoma models, RS5 extended median survival by ~79% without observable liver toxicity.
Dose escalation from 2 mg/kg to 10 mg/kg resulted in fibrotic tumor remnants with no viable cancer cells.
Weekly or bi‑weekly dosing maintained efficacy with stable body weight and normal hematology.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Potential MDR1‑mediated efflux observed in some resistant tumors.
Limited efficacy in brain tumors suggests blood‑brain barrier or vascular remodeling may affect delivery.

combination_strategies.rationale: MMAE sensitizes DNA damage, potentially enhancing radiotherapy efficacy.

open_questions: What is the identity of the TAM‑specific receptor and its expression in normal tissues?
Can MAC‑TACs be combined with checkpoint inhibitors to overcome residual immunosuppression?

talk_title: Collagen‑binding anti‑CTLA‑4 antibodies for recurrent glioblastoma

targets.dependency_context: recurrent GBM with collagen I/III up‑regulation

biomarkers.name: Collagen I
biomarkers.finding: significantly up‑regulated in recurrent GBM vs normal brain

biomarkers.name: Collagen III
biomarkers.finding: significantly up‑regulated in recurrent GBM vs normal brain

MOA_summary: A von Willebrand factor A3 collagen‑binding domain fused to anti‑CTLA‑4 directs the antibody to collagen‑rich regions of recurrent GBM, increasing intratumoral accumulation, depleting exhausted CD8⁺ T cells and reducing Tregs, thereby extending survival in mouse models.
key_findings: Collagen I/III are among the top up‑regulated genes in recurrent GBM compared with primary tumor and normal brain.
CBD anti‑CTLA‑4 shows >5‑fold higher tumor accumulation than wild‑type anti‑CTLA‑4 with no brain accumulation.
In orthotopic GBM mice, CBD anti‑CTLA‑4 increased median survival by 140% and reduced tumor size by day 10.
Ex vivo GBM slice cultures treated with CBD ipilimumab showed significant reduction of intratumoral Tregs.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Partial loss of CD8⁺ T‑cell numbers observed; mechanism unclear.
Potential off‑target liver accumulation noted; ongoing toxicity assessment.

combination_strategies.rationale: CBD anti‑CTLA‑4 may prime T cells for checkpoint blockade synergy.
combination_strategies.phenotype: modest survival benefit in preclinical combos

open_questions: Does collagen binding affect antibody Fc‑mediated functions?
Can this approach be generalized to other checkpoint inhibitors?

talk_title: DLL3‑CD2‑CD3 trispecific T‑cell engager with enhanced potency

models.name: human PBMC‑engrafted mice with SHP77 tumors

MOA_summary: A trispecific antibody simultaneously engages DLL3 on SCLC cells, CD3 for primary T‑cell activation, and CD2 for costimulatory signaling, resulting in enhanced T‑cell proliferation, survival, and cytotoxicity without antigen‑independent activation or T‑cell fratricide.
key_findings: Trispecific TCE shows >100‑fold potency increase over tarlatamab in vitro across multiple donors.
Enhanced T‑cell proliferation and survival observed at sub‑nanomolar concentrations.
In vivo, 5/7 mice achieved complete tumor eradication versus 2/6 with tarlatamab.
No T‑cell bridging or fratricide detected; activation is antigen‑dependent.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Tarlatamab lost activity after serial tumor challenges, whereas trispecific maintained cytotoxicity.
open_questions: Will the CD2 costimulatory domain increase CRS severity in humans?
Can the trispecific format be adapted to other tumor antigens?

talk_title: CBP/P300 bromodomain inhibition synergizes with PARP inhibition in prostate cancer

speaker.name: Orly Richter
speaker.affiliation: Shafi Lab, Center for Prostate Disease Research

biomarkers.name: BRCA1 loss (chr17 deletion)
biomarkers.finding: associated with ex vivo response to CCS1477 + olaparib

models.name: PDEs from 8 African‑American prostate cancer patients

MOA_summary: CBP/P300 bromodomain inhibition down‑regulates homologous recombination and other DNA‑repair pathways, sensitizing CRPC cells to PARP inhibition; combined treatment forces reliance on error‑prone repair, leading to synergistic loss of viability.
key_findings: CBP/P300 are amplified in metastatic CRPC and correlate with poor survival.
CCS1477 suppresses DDR gene expression, especially HR genes, in CRPC cell lines.
Synergy observed between CCS1477 and all four FDA‑approved PARP inhibitors across multiple prostate cancer lines.
In patient‑derived explants, the combination reduced Ki‑67 positivity more than either agent alone in responsive samples.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Non‑responders lacked CNV alterations; mechanism of resistance under investigation.

combination_strategies.rationale: CBP/P300 inhibition impairs HR, creating synthetic lethality with PARP blockade.
combination_strategies.phenotype: moderate to high synergy in viability assays; enhanced Ki‑67 suppression ex vivo

open_questions: Which genomic biomarkers predict response to the combination?
Can the regimen be combined with immune checkpoint blockade?

talk_title: 3D bioprinted cancer models for drug screening

speaker.name: Aurelie Cadoe
speaker.affiliation: Cancer Research Center of Lyon / CRO Anticeneo

models.name: BT474 (HER2+ breast) and SK‑OV‑3 (HER2+ ovarian) constructs
models.genotype_or_key_features: bioink with cancer cells, printed lattice, up to 40 days culture

MOA_summary: Layer‑by‑layer extrusion of cell‑laden bioink creates reproducible 3D tumor constructs that recapitulate in vivo gene expression and drug penetration kinetics, enabling more predictive assessment of chemotherapeutics and ADCs compared with 2D cultures.
key_findings: 3D bioprinted models cluster transcriptionally with in vivo tumors, unlike 2D cultures.
IC₅₀ values for Taxol and TDM‑1 are higher in 3D models, reflecting reduced drug penetration.
Co‑culture with fibroblasts or adipocytes further decreases drug sensitivity.
Intratumoral paclitaxel concentrations in 3D models peak at ~8 h, mirroring mouse plasma kinetics.
open_questions: Can vascular channels be integrated to model perfusion and immune cell infiltration?
How do drug uptake kinetics correlate with long‑term cytotoxic outcomes?
